_source: NASA Eclipse Database (eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov) - Real total solar eclipse data with verified paths of totality
_description: Historical and future total solar eclipses with precise timing and coordinates of greatest eclipse, sourced from NASA Goddard Space Flight Center Five Millennium Canon of Solar Eclipses

total_solar_eclipses.name: Great American Eclipse
total_solar_eclipses.notes: First total solar eclipse visible coast-to-coast across the US since 1918. Path crossed from Oregon to South Carolina.

total_solar_eclipses.name: Great North American Eclipse
total_solar_eclipses.notes: Path crossed Mexico, central US, and eastern Canada. Maximum totality of 4 min 28 sec near Torreon, Mexico.

total_solar_eclipses.name: South American Eclipse
total_solar_eclipses.notes: Visible from parts of Chile and Argentina. La Serena and La Higuera among best viewing sites.

total_solar_eclipses.name: Patagonian Eclipse
total_solar_eclipses.notes: Path crossed southern Chile and Argentina. Total duration at greatest eclipse was 2 min 10 sec.

total_solar_eclipses.name: Arctic Eclipse
total_solar_eclipses.notes: Visible from Arctic Russia, Greenland, Iceland, and northern Spain. First total eclipse visible from Spain since 1905.
